name: TRELLIS.2
description: Local TRELLIS.2 GGUF 3D generation backend packaged as an installable Nymph module.

overview.body:
TRELLIS.2 is Microsoft's open-source 4B image-to-3D model for generating high-fidelity PBR textured 3D assets from images. It uses an O-Voxel representation to handle complex shapes, sharp features, and rich surface materials. This Nymph module installs a local GGUF backend for TRELLIS.2.

FlashAttention install guide:
Install may compile flash-attn from source with the official pip command. GPU sets TRELLIS_FLASH_ATTN_CUDA_ARCHS, which becomes FLASH_ATTN_CUDA_ARCHS for that flash-attn build only. Auto-detect reads the NVIDIA compute capability and narrows the build when possible; manual values are useful if auto-detect is wrong or unavailable.

GPU arch quick guide:
SM80 is Ampere, including RTX 30-series and A100. SM90 is Hopper, including H100 and H800. SM100 is Blackwell, including RTX 50-series and B-series datacenter GPUs. SM110 and SM120 are newer CUDA targets; use them only if you know your toolkit and GPU need them. Max jobs sets MAX_JOBS, while NVCC threads sets NVCC_THREADS inside each compiler job. These options do not change model download size, runtime quality, or normal runtime GPU settings. For 32GB RAM, use Max jobs 4 and NVCC threads 2; drop NVCC threads to 1 if memory pressure is high. Hugging Face token entry appears during Model Fetch after install, because model downloads use the token and FlashAttention install does not.
requirements: Python 3.10 in the managed NymphsCore WSL distro
Enough free RAM for flash-attn native compilation

options.label: Auto-detect
options.description: Read the NVIDIA compute capability and select the flash-attn target arch

options.label: SM80 / Ampere
options.description: Ampere GPUs, including RTX 30-series and A100

options.label: SM90 / Hopper
options.description: Hopper GPUs, including H100 and H800

options.label: SM100 / Blackwell
options.description: Blackwell GPUs, including RTX 50-series and B-series datacenter GPUs

options.label: SM110 / Thor
options.description: Thor-class flash-attn target when supported by the CUDA toolkit

options.label: SM120 / Newer Blackwell
options.description: Newer Blackwell-class flash-attn target when supported by the CUDA toolkit

options.label: MAX_JOBS=2
options.description: Lowest RAM pressure for the flash-attn source build

options.label: MAX_JOBS=4
options.description: Recommended flash-attn build cap for 32GB RAM

options.label: MAX_JOBS=8
options.description: More CPU and RAM use during the flash-attn source build

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=1
options.description: Safest compiler thread setting for lower RAM pressure

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=2
options.description: Moderate compiler threading if the machine has RAM headroom

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=4
options.description: Aggressive compiler threading; can multiply memory pressure with Max jobs

manager_action_groups.title: Model Fetch
manager_action_groups.description:
Install sets up the TRELLIS.2 GGUF backend only. Fetch Models downloads the actual model files.
Every fetch downloads the selected Aero-Ex/Trellis2-GGUF quant plus the required microsoft/TRELLIS.2-4B support checkpoint and the rembg u2net background-removal model.
Start with Q5_K_M for most GPUs. Q4_K_M is smaller and lighter. Q6_K and Q8_0 are larger quality-focused options. Choose All quants only if Blender/addon switching needs every supported GGUF later.

options.label: All quants
options.description: Download every supported GGUF so Blender can switch later

options.label: Q4_K_M
options.description: Smallest option, lower VRAM, fastest first test

options.label: Q6_K
options.description: Higher quality, larger download and more VRAM

options.label: Q8_0
options.description: Largest local GGUF option, best fidelity if the GPU has room

notes: This module preserves the main-branch GGUF quantized-model pull behavior.
Model fetch uses native manager_action_groups and installed module-owned scripts.
Do not commit GGUF model weights, Hugging Face cache, .venv, logs, outputs, or generated assets.
